summary: Pulsing Haptics (iter35) — full frontend verification. All 11 testable acceptance criteria PASSED: (1) haptics-button renders in left-rail header next to ai-companion/install-app/account/logout; (2) modal opens with all 4 pattern cards (auto/heartbeat/breath478/frequency); (3) master toggle flips ON and persists via localStorage 'sf_haptic_prefs_v1' (verified by reload); (4) selecting breath478 updates LS to {"enabled":true,"pattern":"breath478"}; (5) Test-pulse button triggers navigator.vibrate exactly once (payload [90,60,90]); (6) starting playback while enabled triggered navigator.vibrate within 3s (breath478 inhale pulse [400]); pause invoked navigator.vibrate(0); (7) on simulated unsupported device (navigator.vibrate=undefined) the haptics-unsupported notice is visible inside the modal AND audio playback still works (timer started, play button toggled to pause icon, no crash); (8) after pause, NO new vibrate calls in next 2.2s — engine cleanly stops; (9) modal closes via both close button and backdrop click; (10) all four existing header buttons (ai-companion, install-app, account, logout) still render — no regression; (11) backend regression skipped per request (frontend-only review).
critical_code_review_comments: hapticEngine.js & HapticsModal.jsx are clean, well-commented, and follow the singleton + subscriber pattern correctly. No issues observed.
Engine correctly degrades on unsupported devices (typeof navigator.vibrate !== 'function'); audio path is untouched.
_tick() reads live audio state on every recurrence — mid-session frequency/binaural changes propagate without restart. Good.
stop() guards both running and _timer states and always calls navigator.vibrate(0) to cancel any tail — solid.
localStorage key 'sf_haptic_prefs_v1' is versioned — future schema migrations will be straightforward.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pulsing Haptics ships clean. The navigator.vibrate spy strategy (page.add_init_script wrapping navigator.vibrate before navigation, then reading window.__vibCalls) works reliably in Chromium. For the unsupported-device flow, delete navigator.__proto__.vibrate + Object.defineProperty(navigator,'vibrate',{value:undefined}) BEFORE page load so the engine constructor sees supported=false. AI Companion sheet auto-opens after login and must be dismissed via data-testid='ai-agent-close' before clicking header buttons. Engine auto-attaches to audioEngine via Dashboard useEffect — no manual wiring needed during tests.
rca of the issue: No issues found — feature is production-ready.
